questions.question: A Kubernetes Pod is:
options: The smallest deployable unit -- one or more containers sharing network and storage
questions.explanation: Usually one container per pod for ML services.

questions.question: A Kubernetes Deployment manages:
options: Desired state for pods -- ensures N replicas are always running, handles rolling updates
questions.explanation: Deployments self-heal (replace crashed pods) and enable rolling updates.

questions.question: A Kubernetes Service provides:
options: A stable DNS name and IP address for accessing pods (load balancing, service discovery)
questions.explanation: Pods have ephemeral IPs. Services give stable endpoints.

questions.question: Horizontal Pod Autoscaler (HPA) scales based on:
options: CPU/memory utilisation or custom metrics (like prediction request queue depth)
questions.explanation: HPA automatically adds/removes pods to maintain target resource utilisation.

questions.question: Resource requests vs limits in Kubernetes:
options: Requests: minimum guaranteed; Limits: maximum allowed. Scheduling uses requests.
questions.explanation: Scheduler places pods on nodes with sufficient requested resources.

questions.question: kubectl apply -f deployment.yaml:
options: Creates or updates the resource to match the YAML definition (declarative)
questions.explanation: Declarative: specify desired state. kubectl figures out the delta and applies changes.

questions.question: A ConfigMap stores:
options: Non-sensitive configuration data (environment variables, config files) separated from container images
questions.explanation: Separate config from code. Different ConfigMaps for dev/staging/prod.

questions.question: Rolling updates in Kubernetes:
options: Gradually replace old pods with new ones to achieve zero-downtime deployment
questions.explanation: Default update strategy. maxUnavailable and maxSurge control the rollout pace.

questions.question: GPU scheduling in Kubernetes uses:
options: nvidia.com/gpu resource requests -- requires NVIDIA device plugin and GPU nodes
questions.explanation: resources.requests.nvidia.com/gpu: 1 schedules the pod on a GPU node.

questions.question: Kubernetes namespaces provide:
options: Logical partitioning of resources within a cluster for dev/staging/prod separation
questions.explanation: Separate teams or environments in the same cluster. RBAC can be applied per namespace.
